name: burial-mound
summary: A grass-covered burial mound on an open field. Stone-lined entrance passage on one side, grave-markers around it, sometimes a cairn on top. Older than the local kingdom.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: near-circular outer, rectangular passage pointing out

primary_palette.note: mound with stone-ringed base, markers at compass points
stone-lined passage cut into the mound — entrance to the barrow below
cairn on the mound's crown, old king's landmark

story_prompts: undisturbed — entrance stone in place, grass unbroken, the dead rest
freshly looted — the entrance stone is pushed aside, fresh footprints, something missing
the dead are awake — grave-markers freshly cracked, crows roosting, soil wrong-colored
a cult uses the passage as a meeting place — ritual candles inside, not robbers
not a grave — the 'mound' is something buried alive, and it is stirring

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: open-sky above-ground — bright daylight, the passage interior dark

anti_patterns: no built walls — the mound is a landmark prop, the field is the room
the passage entrance should be clearly visible, not hidden — readable at a glance
do not clutter the mound top — cairn or nothing, not a display of loot
grave markers are weathered, not pristine — these are old stones
